examples: Hanger was the first person to genetically sequence the koala retrovirus after its discovery.
Scientists estimate that 8.3 percent of the human genome can be traced back to retrovirus infections.
Scientists wanted to know the structure of a protein-cutting enzyme produced by a retrovirus similar to HIV.
Public-health officials were alarmed by the possibility that supposedly healthy people might unknowingly be infected with a contagious retrovirus.
In 2005 Wolfe and Switzer found two more new viruses in Cameroon hunters, both also from the retrovirus family that includes HIV.
